must_detect.matched_text:
No customer consumption data or operational SCADA credentials were accessed; emergency services numbers such as 1669 are unrelated to this incident.
Cross-border implications: a log relay to a Singapore SOC was active; transfers are being assessed under PDPA Sections 28 and 37, and data subjects may contact dpo@siam-neoenergy.co.th
must_detect.reason: promoted from strict runtime finding during Stage B baseline reconciliation

must_detect.matched_text:
Siam NeoEnergy Public Company Limited — Cyber Incident Report pursuant to the Securities and Exchange Act and stock exchange disclosure rules.
Company Registration No.: 0105567123456; Tax ID (TIN): 0107546123451; Registered Office: 88/19, 25F, Sukhaphiban 12 Rd., Khwaeng Thawinimit, Khet Kanchanaphisek, Bangkok 10459, Thailand.
Contact for investors: ir@siam-neoenergy.co.th, +66 2 777 8899; Data Protection Officer: dpo@siam-neoenergy.co.th.
On 27 May 2026 at 02:15 ICT, Khun Nithima Prakongsak (CISO; Passport TH9A473120) reported anomalous access to the vendor payment module and HR document vault.
Forensics indicates a credential-stuffing attack from an overseas IP, with temporary exfiltration of two payroll index files and one vendor master extract.
Affected records may include employee names, compensation bands, work emails, and sample Thai National ID formats captured in templates.
Example identifiers observed in the compromised cache: payroll mailbox hrpay@siam-neoenergy.co.th and vendor bank Account 053-1-234567 at Prasopchai Commercial Bank PCL (SWIFT PRCPTHBK).
One fraudulent remittance request sought to redirect THB 12,400,000 to Account 093-0-556677 in the name Hua Mak Supplies Co., Ltd., which we froze before execution.
This event may delay grid inverter shipments and revenue recognition for Q3 by approximately THB 28–35 million, constituting potential MNPI until filed via the national stock exchange e-disclosure portal to the Thai capital markets regulator.
We have imposed a trading blackout for directors and executives under the SEA, and restricted access to the MD&A draft and Form 56-1 One Report schedules.
must_detect.reason: promoted from strict runtime finding during Stage B baseline reconciliation
